steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses 'as' to explain why Siemowit's existence is debated: our knowledge comes from dubious oral traditions written centuries later. The blank must express how our knowledge relates to those unreliable sources — specifically, that it originates from them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — unsupported by: states that our knowledge does not come from these sources, which contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — independent of: says our knowledge does not depend on those traditions, which contradicts the passage's claim that our knowledge comes from them. ✗
  • Choice C — derived from: directly states that our knowledge originates in these dubious traditions, which explains why the existence is debated. ✓
  • Choice D — reminiscent of: says our knowledge is merely similar to those traditions, not that it comes from them. ✗
C is correct.
